summary: Gruber performs a line-by-line satirical 'translation' of Apple's official letter addressing iPhone 4 antenna reception complaints. He decodes the corporate PR language into what he believes Apple is actually thinking — frustration that a genuinely improved antenna design is being overshadowed by one flaw, and that their misleading signal bar formula has backfired. He acknowledges the real attenuation issue while arguing the iPhone 4 is still a net improvement in reception. The piece captures Apple's exasperation at the media firestorm and anticipates the backlash that will follow the software fix.
key_points: Apple's signal bar formula was deliberately set to display inflated reception, and that decision backfired when users noticed dramatic bar drops from the antenna grip issue
The iPhone 4 antenna is actually a net improvement — faster downloads, 10x faster uploads, fewer dropped calls — but the one step back is getting all the attention
The software fix will only change bar display, not actual reception, but Gruber predicts media outlets will claim the update made reception worse
The real attenuation from the antenna gap grip is about 20-30 percent signal loss, which Apple plans to mask by recalibrating the bar algorithm
Apple's tone throughout the letter is corporate restraint barely concealing genuine frustration at the disproportionate media response
money_quotes: We cannot believe we had to write this fucking letter.
It's like a two steps forward, one step back design, except maybe more like three steps forward.

We decided from the outset to set the formula for our bars-of-signal strength indicator to make the iPhone look good — to make it look as if it 'gets more bars'. That decision has now bitten us on our ass.

We are braced for the backlash when, after installing this update, people who weren't experiencing any problems at all with their iPhones start complaining, loudly, that their phones which used to get five bars now only get three or two.
Don't fuck this thing up for us. We mean, have you seen the Retina Display?
Take your class action suits filed four days after we released the goddamn thing and stick them up your fucking asses.
Don't hold it that way, or buy a case.
key_insight: Apple's iPhone 4 antenna controversy was amplified by their own misleading signal bar formula — a vanity decision that turned a minor hardware tradeoff into a PR crisis.
